directory.configure_ldap: Configure an LDAP server in Settings to use the Directory.
settings.saved_restart_notice: Saved — restart DeeLip to apply changes.
settings.random_popup_hint: Show the main window at a random spot on the current monitor each time it's raised for an incoming call, instead of wherever it last was.
settings.default_list_action_hint: What double-clicking a row's name/number in History or Contacts does. "Edit" falls back to "Call" in History (nothing to edit there).
settings.single_call_mode_hint: An incoming call while another is already active is rejected with Busy instead of ringing as a 2nd call. A per-account "Forward on busy" (Account editor) still takes priority over this.
settings.log_to_file_hint: Also writes logs to ~/.config/deelip/deelip.log, in addition to the console. Restart to apply.
settings.crash_reporting_hint: If DeeLip crashes, save a report (version, panic message, backtrace) to ~/.config/deelip/crashes/ for troubleshooting. Purely local -- never uploaded or sent anywhere. Restart to apply.
settings.account.accounts_hint: Each enabled account registers independently on its own local SIP port (base port below, incrementing by one per additional account).
settings.account.enabled_checkbox: Enabled (register this account on next restart)
settings.account.dnd_checkbox: Do Not Disturb (reject all incoming calls)
settings.account.local_account_hint: Place and receive calls straight to/from an IP address with no SIP server at all -- no REGISTER is ever sent. Server, Password, Login, and Transport below are ignored (always plain UDP); dial a bare IP or host[:port] (e.g. 192.168.1.50 or 192.168.1.50:5060) directly from the dialer. Username/Display name are still used as this account's caller-ID identity. Restart required.
settings.account.login_info: Digest-auth identity, when a provider requires a login distinct from the public SIP username above.
settings.account.domain_info: SIP domain used in From/To/Contact URIs, when it differs from the registrar host in Server above.
settings.account.sip_proxy_info: Outbound proxy to actually connect through, instead of Server/Port directly.
settings.account.transport_auto_info: Tries UDP, then TCP, then TLS at connect time, keeping whichever one actually gets a response from the server.
settings.account.tls_warning: Warning: certificate verification is disabled — traffic can be intercepted.
settings.account.force_incoming_codec_in: Negotiates this codec on an incoming call whenever the caller offers it at all, ignoring the caller's own preference order.
settings.account.vad_info: During silence, sends occasional comfort-noise packets instead of continuous audio, and plays synthesized background noise for the far end's silence instead of dead air. Only takes effect with a non-Opus codec.
settings.account.dtmf_auto: Auto (RFC 2833 if negotiated, else SIP INFO)
settings.account.auto_answer_info: Answers any incoming call on this account after the timer below, regardless of who's calling -- distinct from Auto Answer (Control Button) below, which only fires on a specific remote paging signal.
settings.account.auto_answer_control_inf: Auto-answer only when the incoming INVITE itself carries a remote paging/intercom signal (a Call-Info: ...;answer-after=N header, as sent by door-intercom/paging hardware) -- unlike the timer above, this doesn't fire on an ordinary call and bypasses DND/forwarding when it does fire.
settings.account.deny_incoming_info: Reacts to the same remote paging/intercom signal as Auto Answer (Control Button) above, but rejects the call instead. Takes priority if both are somehow enabled.
settings.account.mailbox_info: Extension/mailbox this account subscribes to for Message-Waiting-Indicator (MWI) NOTIFY -- new-voicemail count shown as the badge next to the status bar. Leave blank to skip MWI subscription entirely.
settings.account.publish_presence_info: Publishes this account's own availability (open/closed, following Do Not Disturb) via PUBLISH -- needs a server with a presence agent that accepts it. Restart required to apply.
settings.account.dialing_prefix_info: Auto-prepended to bare numbers dialed from this account (e.g. "9" for an outside line) -- not applied to a full SIP URI or an explicit user@host entry. Only used as a fallback when no Dial Plan rule below matches.
settings.account.dial_plan_info: Ordered regex match/replace rules applied to a bare dialed number before the Dialing prefix fallback above -- the first enabled rule whose pattern matches wins. E.g. pattern "^0(\d+)$", replacement "$1" strips a leading trunk-access 0.
settings.account.dial_plan_empty: No dial plan rules -- falls back to the prefix above.
settings.account.hide_caller_id_info: Requests the server withhold your identity from the callee -- only effective if the server/provider actually honors Privacy: id; this app can't force it on an uncooperative server.
settings.account.register_refresh_info: Requested REGISTER Expires -- the server may return a shorter value, which re-registration timing always honors regardless of this.
settings.account.session_timers_info: Periodic re-INVITE keep-alives so a dead signaling path (no BYE ever arrives) can still be detected. On by default; disabling sends no Session-Expires/Min-SE at all.
settings.account.nat_keepalive_info: Sends a lone empty packet to the registrar on this interval, to hold a NAT/firewall's outbound binding open between registrations.
settings.account.media_encryption_info: "Match transport" offers SRTP exactly when the signaling transport is TLS (today's behavior); the other two are independent of transport. ZRTP is a from-scratch implementation, verified only against itself (two DeeLip instances) in this codebase's own test suite -- not against any other ZRTP client. Not supported in conference calls (falls back to no encryption for the merged call).
settings.account.video_enabled_info: Offers/accepts a video leg (H.264, 640x480 @15fps) alongside audio for calls on this account. Needs a working camera (see the Video section below) to send video; you can still receive and view the other party's video without one. Not supported in conference calls.
settings.account.public_address_info: Overrides the address advertised in Contact/SDP for this account, instead of the globally STUN-discovered external IP.
settings.account.allow_ip_rewrite_info: Rewrites the advertised Contact/SDP IP from the registrar's own received= feedback on each (re-)registration -- a STUN-free way to self-discover a public address. Ignored while Public address is set.
settings.account.ice_override_checkbox: Override global ICE setting for this account
settings.account.ice_override_info: Lets this one account use a different ICE setting than the global one in Network below -- e.g. disable ICE for a local-only PBX while keeping it on for other accounts.
settings.account.no_accounts_warning: Warning: no accounts are enabled — DeeLip won't be able to register on restart.
settings.audio.ringing_device_hint: Independent of the Output device above -- lets the ringtone play on a different device than call audio, e.g. ring on speakers, talk on a headset.
settings.audio.agc_hint: Adaptively boosts a quiet mic signal and limits a loud one.
settings.audio.record_calls_hint: Saves locally to ~/.config/deelip/recordings by default (see Save to below) -- never uploaded anywhere. Check your local laws on call-recording consent before enabling.
settings.video.camera_hint: Only affects outgoing video -- receiving and displaying the other party's video works with no camera at all. Has no effect unless Enable video (H.264) is also on for the account placing/answering the call.
settings.video.no_cameras: No cameras detected -- video calls will still receive and display the other party's video without one.
settings.network.local_sip_port_hint: Base port this app binds for signaling. Each additional enabled account (Accounts above) uses the next port up. Restart required to apply.
settings.network.stun_server_info: Discovers your public IP/port for NAT traversal -- used as ICE's fallback (or directly, if ICE above is off).
settings.network.turn_server_info: Relay server used when direct/STUN NAT traversal fails (e.g. symmetric NAT on both ends). Needs the Username/Password below if the server requires auth.
settings.network.custom_nameserver_info: DNS server used for SIP server / SRV lookups, instead of the OS-configured resolver.
settings.network.dns_srv_checkbox: Use DNS SRV records to locate the SIP server
settings.network.dns_srv_hint: Looks up _sip._udp/_tcp or _sips._tcp for each account's server host before falling back to a plain A/AAAA lookup. Restart required to apply.
settings.network.ice_checkbox: Use ICE (RFC 8445) for NAT traversal, falling back to the above if it fails
settings.network.ice_hint: Takes effect on the next call placed or answered, not calls already in progress.
settings.network.restrict_rtp_checkbox: Restrict RTP to a port range
settings.network.restrict_rtp_hint: Pin RTP media to a fixed port range for firewall/NAT port-forwarding, instead of an OS-assigned port every call. Restart required to apply.
settings.directory.section_hint: Corporate/LDAP directory lookup, shown in the Directory tab -- read-only search, never writes back to the directory. Restart required to apply.
settings.directory.bind_dn_info: Leave blank for an anonymous bind, if the directory allows unauthenticated search.
settings.directory.use_tls_hint: Connect via implicit TLS instead of plain ldap://.
settings.directory.search_filter_info: "{query}" is replaced with the (escaped) search text. Empty: falls back to a built-in filter matching cn/displayName/mail/sn/givenName.
settings.hotkeys.enable_global_checkbox: Enable system-wide Answer/Hangup/Mute hotkeys (Linux: X11 only)
settings.hotkeys.format_hint: Format: "Ctrl+Alt+A" style. Restart required to apply.
settings.hotkeys.media_buttons_checkbox: Handle Media Buttons (headset Play/Pause answers/hangs up)
settings.hotkeys.media_buttons_hint: Independent of the toggle above -- grabs the hardware media Play/Pause key (Linux: X11 only) to answer a ringing call or hang up the active one, like a headset's hook button. Restart required to apply.
settings.advanced.auto_update_hint: Only works for a portable (tar.gz/install.sh) install -- .deb/.rpm installs are always updated through your package manager instead, regardless of this toggle.
settings.advanced.update_idle: Up to date (or not checked yet).
update.cant_auto_update: This install can't be updated automatically -- use your package manager or re-run install.sh.
update.updated_message: Updated to {version} -- restart DeeLip to finish.
status.remote_held: Remote party put you on hold
status.on_hold: On hold — tap Resume to continue
dialer.zrtp_sas_hover: Read this 4-character code out loud with the other party to confirm no one is intercepting this call.
messages.title: Messages
